title: Acoustic aware voice user interface
abstract: A method for optimal configuration of a voice user interface is disclosed herein. The method includes receiving an audio signal; processing the audio signal by a context awareness module to generate context information regarding an acoustic environment of the audio signal; determining, based on the context information, an optimal one of a plurality of different configurations of a voice user interface to perform voice user interface processing of the audio signal; and performing the voice user interface processing of the audio signal using the optimal configuration of the plurality of different configurations.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method for improving operation of a voice user interface, comprising: receiving an audio signal; generating, by a context awareness module, context information regarding an acoustic environment of the audio signal; determining, based on the context information, an optimal one of a plurality of different configurations of a voice user interface to perform processing of the audio signal; and performing the processing of the audio signal using the optimal configuration of the plurality of different configurations. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein each of the plurality of different configurations is a respective selection for performing the processing by a plurality of different computing resources, the plurality of different computing resources including two or more of a digital signal processor (DSP), an application-specific integrated circuit (ASIC), an Application Processor (AP), or a cloud computing platform. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the context information includes one or more of signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) information, type of background noises, room acoustics, location characteristics, or device operational status. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the plurality of different configurations is a plurality of different statistical models for processing of the audio signal, wherein each model of the plurality of statistical models is trained for a different acoustic environment. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the plurality of different configurations is a plurality of different operation parameters for processing of the audio signal. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the operation parameters include voice activity detection threshold values. 7. The method of claim 1, further comprising: collecting additional information by the context awareness module to be included in the context information. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the additional information includes information from one or more of an inertial sensor, a temperature sensor, a proximity sensor, a device operational status sensor, and a GPS module. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein the voice user interface processing includes one of voice activity detection, keyword detection, voice user authentication, and automatic speech recognition. 10. A system for audio processing, comprising: a microphone configured to capture an audio signal from an acoustic environment; a context awareness module configured to process the audio signal to generate context information regarding the acoustic environment; and a voice user interface configured to determine, based on the context information, an optimal one of a plurality of different configurations to perform voice user interface processing of the audio signal. 11. The system of claim 10, wherein the plurality of different configurations is a plurality of different computing resources, the system further comprising a signal interface configured to transmit the audio signal to the computing resource for voice user interface processing of the audio signal. 12. The system of claim 11, wherein the plurality of computing resources include a digital signal processor (DSP), an application-specific integrated circuit (ASIC), an Application Processor (AP), or a cloud computing platform. 13. The system of claim 12, wherein the cloud computing platform is remotely connected with the system via a network. 14. The system of claim 10, wherein the context information includes signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) information, type of background noises, room acoustics, room characteristics, or background sound type. 15. The system of claim 10, wherein the plurality of different configurations is a plurality of different statistical models, and wherein each model of the plurality of statistical models is trained for a different type of acoustic environment. 16. The system of claim 10, wherein the plurality of different configurations is a plurality of different operation parameters for performing voice user interface processing of the audio signal. 17. The system of claim 16, wherein the operation parameters include voice activity detection threshold values. 18. The system of claim 10, wherein the voice user interface is further configured to collect additional information to be included in the context information, and wherein the additional information includes a sensor information or a device operational status. 19. The system of claim 10, wherein the voice user interface processing includes one of voice activity detection, keyword detection, voice user authentication, and automatic speech recognition.
